quick_reference.logline: Presumed dead for five years, the true Hale heiress returns under a false name and weaponizes public space to dismantle the people who stole her life.
quick_reference.visual_identity_line: Severe black silhouettes in overlit public arenas where status is performed and shattered.
quick_reference.protagonist_snapshot: Nora never retreats — she turns the room into a courtroom and lets the evidence speak.

world_background.power_structure: The Hale world runs on performative legitimacy — public witness, donor networks, and corporate alliances. Whoever controls the room controls the narrative. Private truth is worthless until it lands in front of witnesses.
world_background.social_rules: Status must be constantly performed and publicly defended. Backing down in public is permanent defeat. The person who stays composed under attack is assumed to be right.
world_background.the_core_wound: Nora was erased — her identity stolen, her death staged — so that wealth could be transferred before she could claim it. The system rewarded the theft.
world_background.tone_summary: Cold luxury revenge: controlled devastation delivered in evening wear, in front of cameras.

timeline.event: Amelia Hale, the family matriarch, dies under suspicious circumstances and leaves her estate in a trust requiring her biological heir to claim it by age 30.
timeline.dramatic_impact: Establishes the inheritance mechanism that makes Nora's identity worth stealing.

timeline.event: Nora is involved in a staged car accident arranged by her stepmother Diane and stepsister Celia. She survives but is assumed dead.
timeline.dramatic_impact: The inciting wound — introduces the conspiracy and raises the question of who orchestrated it.

timeline.event: Celia is declared the sole Hale heiress. Adrian Hale, Nora's estranged half-brother, publicly accepts the transition without resistance.
timeline.dramatic_impact: Sets up Celia as usurper and Adrian as the ambiguous insider — ally or accomplice?

timeline.event: Nora, under the alias Eva Stone, builds a network of legal allies and collects documentation proving the inheritance fraud.
timeline.dramatic_impact: Nora enters the story with a plan already in motion — she is never reactive, only strategic.

timeline.event: Eva Stone is introduced as a London-based auction consultant hired to appraise a Hale estate item. She is in the same room as Celia for the first time in five years.
timeline.dramatic_impact: The story begins in public, with Nora already holding one piece of evidence, and Celia not knowing she's in danger.

character_profiles.name: Nora Hale / Eva Stone
character_profiles.public_identity: Eva Stone — high-end auction consultant recently returned from London
character_profiles.hidden_identity: Nora Hale — the biological heir to the Hale estate, presumed dead
character_profiles.core_desire: Reclaim her name, her company, and her grandmother's legacy — in public, on record
character_profiles.core_fear: Being exposed before the evidence chain is complete, losing the element of surprise
character_profiles.fatal_flaw: She trusts the plan more than people — she will sacrifice emotional connection to protect the strategy
character_profiles.character_arc: Solitary weapon with nothing to lose → woman who chooses to rebuild rather than only destroy
signature_behaviors: She straightens the item in front of her — a glass, a document, a chair — before responding to provocation
She never raises her voice; she lowers it
She makes eye contact with the most powerful person in the room first, before addressing anyone else
character_profiles.visual_design: Tall, lean, early 30s. Angular features that read as cold in low emotion and lethal when she smiles. Severe posture — never slouches, never fidgets.

character_profiles.name: Celia Hale
character_profiles.public_identity: The Hale heiress — poised, charitable, socially beloved
character_profiles.hidden_identity: Knows the accident was staged; benefits from Nora's erasure; increasingly aware the past is returning
character_profiles.core_desire: Keep what she has — the name, the money, the room's approval
character_profiles.core_fear: Public exposure — not guilt, but the audience watching her fall
character_profiles.fatal_flaw: She performs strength instead of having it — when the audience turns, she collapses
character_profiles.character_arc: Secure usurper who believes she has won → exposed, publicly dismantled, stripped of audience and identity
signature_behaviors: She touches her pearl bracelet when she lies
She laughs one beat too early — performing delight before she's processed the situation
She positions herself at the center of group photos and social arrangements
character_profiles.visual_design: Mid 30s. Conventionally beautiful, polished presentation. Always slightly over-styled — every detail curated for maximum social approval.

character_profiles.name: Adrian Cole
character_profiles.public_identity: Independent investor and former Hale family legal counsel
character_profiles.hidden_identity: Secretly investigated the accident five years ago and suppressed the findings — he doesn't know if he was protecting Nora or complicit in burying her
character_profiles.core_desire: Absolve himself — prove he tried to protect Nora rather than betray her
character_profiles.core_fear: That Nora already knows what he did and has come back to destroy him too
character_profiles.fatal_flaw: He resolves moral crises by choosing the option that preserves his own position
character_profiles.character_arc: Morally compromised observer who avoided accountability → forced to choose sides in public with everything on the line
signature_behaviors: He goes still when he's computing risk — no expression, no movement for two to three seconds
He asks a question instead of making a statement when he already knows the answer
He positions himself near exits in social settings
character_profiles.visual_design: Late 30s. Well-built, dark formal wear. Handsome in a way that reads as trustworthy until you notice his eyes are always calculating.

character_profiles.name: Diane Hale
character_profiles.public_identity: Hale family matriarch by marriage — gracious, philanthropic
character_profiles.hidden_identity: Architect of the inheritance fraud and the staged accident
character_profiles.core_desire: Maintain control of the Hale estate and ensure Celia's permanent claim
character_profiles.core_fear: A piece of physical evidence she has not yet located and destroyed
character_profiles.fatal_flaw: She underestimates people she has already hurt — she doesn't believe they come back
character_profiles.character_arc: Untouchable orchestrator who believes the past is sealed → systematically exposed from the outside in
signature_behaviors: She smooths the tablecloth or adjusts a centerpiece when she's losing control of a situation
She uses first names in every sentence to assert social ownership of a conversation
She never visibly reacts to bad news in public — she absorbs it and responds in private
character_profiles.visual_design: Early 60s. Elegant, silver-haired, always dressed as if photographed. Movements are deliberate and controlled — she is a performance.

relationship_matrix.relationship_type: Usurped heir vs. usurper — public rivals masking a five-year vendetta
relationship_matrix.hidden_tension: Celia doesn't know Eva is Nora. Nora knows exactly who Celia is and has been waiting.

relationship_matrix.relationship_type: Forced professional proximity escalating toward trust and romantic tension
relationship_matrix.hidden_tension: Nora doesn't know how much Adrian knows. Adrian doesn't know if Nora is here to take him down too.

relationship_matrix.relationship_type: Target and hunter — Nora has Diane in her sights but must not show it yet
relationship_matrix.hidden_tension: Diane senses something familiar about Eva but cannot place it. Nora is waiting for the right moment to make it unmistakable.

relationship_matrix.relationship_type: Mother-daughter alliance built on shared complicity
relationship_matrix.hidden_tension: Celia is starting to fear Diane would sacrifice her to protect herself if the exposure came.

key_garments: high-neck fitted black dress or trouser
look_profiles.hair_and_makeup: Low chignon or severe straight blowout. Skin: matte, no warmth. Lip: deep berry or clean nude. Eyes: precise liner, restrained.
look_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: female late 20s, severe black satin power blazer, high-neck fitted black dress, sharp angular silhouette, single small gold earring, controlled low chignon, cool skin tone, matte makeup, cold confidence, studio editorial lighting with harsh top light, fashion portrait, 4K

look_profiles.episodes_or_contexts: Early episodes when Nora must not draw attention, Episodes 1-8
look_profiles.hair_and_makeup: Hair down in soft waves — less severe than Power Black. Slightly warmer makeup, approachable but professional.
look_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: female late 20s, charcoal structured coat over cream silk blouse, dark slim trousers, hair in loose waves, professional but understated, consultancy office environment, soft directional lighting, photorealistic, 4K

look_profiles.episodes_or_contexts: All social events and public appearances, Episodes 1-40
look_profiles.hair_and_makeup: Polished updo with soft face-framing pieces. Dewy skin, rose-gold palette — warm, approachable, curated for photographs.
look_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: female mid 30s, champagne evening gown, pearl bracelet, warm rose-gold makeup, soft updo with loose pieces, polished social event styling, elegant ballroom background, warm fill light, photorealistic portrait, 4K

look_profiles.episodes_or_contexts: All professional and social appearances, Episodes 1-40
look_profiles.hair_and_makeup: Clean professional cut. No visible makeup. Groomed but not styled — deliberately leaving room for ambiguity.
look_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: male late 30s, navy bespoke suit, clean professional appearance, calculating expression, slight distance in the eyes, formal event or boardroom setting, mixed directional lighting, photorealistic portrait, 4K

key_garments: dove grey or silver structured jacket
look_profiles.hair_and_makeup: Silver-white hair, always perfectly set. Skin flawless, makeup invisible but present. Everything about her says she has never made a mistake in public.
look_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: female early 60s, dove grey structured jacket, silver-white hair perfectly set, invisible but immaculate makeup, aristocratic bearing, old-money elegance, formal interior setting, cool diffused lighting, photorealistic portrait, 4K

location_profiles.name: Hale Charity Gala Ballroom
location_profiles.dramatic_function: Primary arena for public status performance and dismantling — Nora's key battleground
location_profiles.lighting_mood: Harsh overhead event lighting that exposes rather than flatters. No warmth. Shadows are sharp. The room is a stage.
location_profiles.sound_environment: Live string quartet, crystal glasses, controlled murmur of social performance — silence cuts through when a scene lands
location_profiles.continuity_notes: Same chandelier arrangement and marble floor in every appearance. The main entrance doors must always be visible from the primary stage position.
location_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: luxury charity gala ballroom interior, crystal chandeliers, cold white marble floor, black and gold color scheme, harsh overhead lighting, elegant but emotionally hostile, event photography style, wide establishing shot, 4K

location_profiles.name: Nora's Private Apartment
location_profiles.dramatic_function: The only space where Nora is not performing — where strategy is built and vulnerability is allowed
location_profiles.lighting_mood: Cool blue-white task lighting. Single pendant light over the work table. No ambient warmth — this is a command center, not a home.
location_profiles.sound_environment: Near silence. Low hum of a city outside. Occasional paper shuffle. Music only when Nora is processing emotion alone.
location_profiles.continuity_notes: The evidence board on the east wall must always be partially visible but never fully legible in wide shots. The same notebook must appear on the table in every scene.
location_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: modern minimal apartment interior, cool blue-white lighting, sparse furniture, one work table with documents and a notebook, evidence board partially visible on wall, no warmth, photorealistic interior, wide angle, 4K

location_profiles.name: Hale Corporation Boardroom
location_profiles.dramatic_function: Power legitimacy arena — where corporate control is asserted and the inheritance battle plays out formally
location_profiles.lighting_mood: Natural daylight from floor-to-ceiling windows, slightly cold. Overhead panels add institutional flatness. Power is environmental, not warm.
location_profiles.sound_environment: Silence with weight. Air conditioning hum. No music — every word lands in empty space.
location_profiles.continuity_notes: City skyline outside the windows must always appear at day or dusk — never full night (that is the gala location's domain). The same chairs and table configuration in every scene.
location_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: corporate boardroom interior, long dark conference table, floor-to-ceiling windows, city skyline, cold grey and steel palette, institutional authority, natural daylight, photorealistic interior, wide shot, 4K

prop_profiles.name: Amelia Hale Family Seal
prop_profiles.visual_description: A heavy engraved metal crest, approximately 6cm diameter, housed in a navy velvet-lined case. The engraving shows an intertwined HH monogram with a crown. The metal has the patina of age — not decorative gold, but real old metal.
continuity_rules: The seal must always appear identical — same patina, same case, same engravings
The velvet case lining is navy, never black — this distinction matters for close-up shots
The seal is always handled by Nora using both hands — never one-handed
prop_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: antique family seal crest, engraved metal HH monogram with crown, aged patina, heavy authoritative object, housed in navy velvet case, still life product photography, dramatic single light source, dark background, 4K macro

prop_profiles.name: The Suppressed Report
prop_profiles.visual_description: A physical document — legal-sized pages in a plain manila envelope, labeled only with a case number. The pages are slightly worn at the edges, suggesting they have been held and put away many times.
continuity_rules: The envelope must always show the same handwritten case number in the same position
Pages inside are never fully legible on camera — only partial text visible in close-up
The envelope is always slightly crumpled — it has been handled under stress
prop_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: legal document manila envelope, slightly worn edges, handwritten case number on front, isolated on dark surface, dramatic side lighting, still life, photorealistic, 4K

prop_profiles.name: Celia's Pearl Bracelet
prop_profiles.visual_description: A delicate single-strand pearl bracelet with a small gold clasp. Traditional and expensive — the kind of thing given by a family to mark an occasion. Worn on the left wrist.
continuity_rules: Always on Celia's left wrist in every scene she appears in
When Celia is under severe stress, the bracelet may be absent — this signals her performance has broken
The bracelet must never look fake or costume — always high-quality pearl
prop_profiles.ai_prompt_seed: delicate single-strand pearl bracelet on feminine wrist, small gold clasp, elegant jewelry detail, studio macro photography, clean white or dark background, soft directional light, photorealistic, 4K

visual_system.lighting_philosophy: Harsh top light and cold fill — nothing is softened. Public spaces are over-lit like a courtroom. Private spaces have single-source cold task light. There is no warmth in this world except what characters perform.

hero_vs_villain_contrast.hero_visual: Nora: severe black silhouette, minimal surface, controlled negative space — reads as force through restraint
hero_vs_villain_contrast.villain_visual: Celia: warm saturated palette, maximum surface decoration, high information density — reads as desperation dressed as confidence

forbidden_aesthetics: warm filter or golden hour glow on any scene
any shot that makes Celia look vulnerable or sympathetic before Episode 30

continuity_rules.rule_description: Nora's small crescent scar is always on the inside of her left wrist. It is never covered by clothing in close-up shots and must appear in the same position across all appearances.
Nora's Power Black look must never include warm-toned accessories. Gold is permitted only as minimal hardware (earrings, clasp). No red, no warm brown, no champagne.
The Hale family seal must always appear visually identical across all appearances — same patina, same navy velvet case. Any color or material deviation is a continuity error.

Celia must always read visually warmer, brighter, and more decorated than Nora in any shared scene. If they appear together, Celia's color temperature and surface decoration must be measurably higher.
The gala ballroom must always show the same chandelier arrangement and marble floor. Camera angles may vary but the physical space must be visually consistent.
Celia's pearl bracelet must be on her left wrist in every scene she appears in, except scenes where her public performance has broken — bracelet absence is a deliberate signal, not an oversight.

Before Episode 10, Nora must never be visually recognizable as Nora Hale to other characters. Any framing that lets in-world characters see the scar too clearly before the story's reveal window is a timing error.

narrative_forbidden_zones: Nora must never beg, cry first, or physically retreat from a confrontation — she may be wounded but she does not break in front of an enemy.
The family seal must never appear damaged, incomplete, or different from its established visual description.
Celia must not be portrayed sympathetically or as a victim until after Episode 30 — her suffering must be clearly a consequence of her own choices, not audience manipulation.
Adrian must never be definitively established as innocent or guilty before Episode 25 — his ambiguity is a core dramatic engine.
No character may reveal that they know Eva is Nora before the story's designed reveal window without being given a specific scene that seals their silence.
